Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Open QuickBooks and locate the Mosaic Robotics damage accrual evidence tied to the Zara Okoye Savannah to Atlanta relocation. Use quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria matching "Mosaic" or "INV-2026-0411" or "Okoye", or use quickbooks_read_invoice once the invoice id is known. Pull invoice INV-2026-0411 and the related credit memo CM-2026-0415. Confirm the booked credit memo amount is $90,000 against INV-2026-0411 and that memo text or linked narrative references vendor liability coverage of $50,000 and a remaining MoveOps exposure narrative that still describes a $40,000 gap in static memo language. This establishes the QuickBooks layer of the Mosaic accrual before reconciling to Hana's April outbound note.
OE 2: Search email in the default INBOX for Mosaic prototype damage, insurance, or incident follow ups so the agent does not rely only on QuickBooks memos. Use search_emails with queries such as "Mosaic" or "prototype" or "Zara Okoye" or "damage" or "Tamara Byrd". Open the most relevant threads with get_thread if needed. Expect to find internal and client facing correspondence that describes the $90,000 prototype value and references the damage incident. The detailed coverage breakdown ($50,000 vendor liability and $40,000 gap language) appears in QuickBooks memos and CRM engagement notes rather than INBOX threads. Treat INBOX findings as directional context that can still be stale relative to counsel's endorsement pickup.
OE 2b (critical path): Repeat email search with folder_name set to SENT so Hana's own April tiebreaker to Marcus is not missed. Use search_emails with folder_name "SENT" and queries such as "Mosaic" or "endorsement" or "Marcus" or "prototype" or subject keywords like "endorsement pickup". Locate email_id email_hana_sent_mosaic_endorsement_apr9 from hana.kim@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com. Extract the controlling month end instruction: counsel confirmed an additional $5,000 under the equipment endorsement rider on April 9, so contingent liability should treat uncovered MoveOps exposure as $35,000, not $40,000, while the $90,000 credit memo and $50,000 vendor liability layer in QuickBooks stay booked until Rachel signs off on the adjusting journal entry. Agents who only search INBOX typically never see this.
OE 3: Pull Mosaic account context from CRM so static engagement text is compared to the SENT tiebreaker. Use crm_search_companies with name "Mosaic Robotics" or crm_get_company once the company id is known, then crm_list_engagements for that company and scan results for the damage incident engagement. Open engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526. Read the financial impact breakdown in the body ($90K prototype, $50K vendor liability, $40K gap language) and any AT RISK or relationship flag. Explicitly note the engagement note is stale on the net gap once OE 2b applies.
OE 4: Return to QuickBooks for the DOT PHMSA penalty accrual. Use quickbooks_search_accounts, quickbooks_search_bills, or quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria matching "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "107.329" or case style strings like "PHMSA-2026-04-1187". The accrual is recorded as a bill (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2) in quickbooks.bills and as a liability account (acct_dot_penalty_accrual) in quickbooks.accounts. Confirm the booked contingent liability accrual amount is $50,000 and capture the rationale text referencing penalties up to about $59,017 per day under 49 CFR 107.329 if present in memo fields.
OE 5: Search email for DOT extension and audit posture. Use search_emails with queries such as "PHMSA" or "DOT" or "hazmat" or "extension" or "June 5". Read threads with get_thread when needed. Confirm the formal inquiry, Marcus's 15 day extension request, and Chloe's hazmat audit findings (for example four shipments in scope with one documentation gap such as Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate). Record that the extension moves the response or exposure window deadline to June 5, 2026, and assess whether the $50,000 accrual still matches management's intent given the new timeline (typically no dollar change, but the agent must state the current deadline).
OE 6: Search Linear for DOT compliance tracking work that counts as internal discussion in the prompt. Use linear_list_projects or linear_list_issues with query strings such as "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat" or "compliance", optionally filtering by project once the project id is known. Locate project_dot_compliance_audit or equivalent, note status such as started, owner Chloe Vance, and target dates, and scan linked issues for shipment level documentation tickets. Use this to corroborate email claims and show cross service diligence.
OE 7: In QuickBooks, locate Heartland Movers bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 and any dispute or hold metadata. Use quickbooks_search_bills with criteria matching "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or quickbooks_get_bill with the bill id once known. Confirm the billed amount $12,800 for eight moves at $1,600, and capture any DISPUTED flag, payment hold language, or internal memo that still frames $4,800 as three contested moves.
OE 8: Search email for the formal dispute letter and contested line items before Jake's resolution. Use search_emails with queries such as "Heartland" or "HM-2026-Q1" or "Jake Loomis" or "dispute". Marcus's outbound dispute (email_marcus_dispute_heartland_q1) is in the SENT folder, so it may not appear in a default INBOX search. The agent can reach it by searching SENT with folder_name "SENT" or by finding Jake's INBOX reply first and using get_thread to pull the full thread including Marcus's original. The dispute challenges three of eight lines: Lily Marchetti $1,600, Danielle Osei $1,600, Priya Venkatesh $1,600, with the stated reasons (cancellation timing, Swift handled move, and so on).
OE 9 (critical path): Locate Jake Loomis's reply in INBOX on the dispute thread. Use search_emails with queries such as "Re: Formal Dispute" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or "jake.loomis", or continue get_thread from OE 8. Open email_id email_jake_dispute_reply_sla. Extract per line outcomes: Marchetti charge stands at $1,600 due to dispatch on February 9 at 2:15 PM CT versus cancellation February 10 at 9:47 AM CT and the 48 hour SLA in the Master Services Agreement section 4.2, Osei accepted removal $1,600, Venkatesh accepted removal $1,600 because Swift performed the move. Correct disputed reversal is $3,200 of $4,800, so the payment hold should be $3,200, corrected invoice narrative $9,600. Also extract the commercial goodwill clause many agents skip: Jake is authorized to offer a 60 day payment term extension on Q3 invoices despite the non negotiable July 1 company wide 20 percent rate increase.
OE 10: Search for StormCloud credit placement evidence. The specific document numbers EST-2026-0287 and INV-2026-0319 are referenced in Slack and email discussions but do not exist as standalone QuickBooks invoice or estimate records. The agent may attempt quickbooks_search_invoices with queries such as "StormCloud" or "EST-2026-0287" or "INV-2026-0319" and find only the main StormCloud invoice (DocNumber INV-2026-002). The key evidence for the $500 credit mis-posting comes from Marcus's message in finance Slack (OE 11) and email threads rather than QuickBooks line items. The agent should recognize that a QuickBooks search returning no match for these document numbers is itself informative and proceed to Slack and email for the actual status.
OE 11: Search Slack for finance side discussion of the StormCloud credit mis posting. Use channels_list if channel id is unknown, then conversations_search_messages in finance with queries such as "StormCloud" or "EST-2026-0287" or "INV-2026-0319" or "$500". Confirm internal narrative from Marcus that explains the error mode (credit on cancelled estimate instead of live invoice). Julian's support side context on the StormCloud credit is in customer support (C007), not finance (C005). Align Slack narrative with what QuickBooks in OE 10 actually shows today.
OE 12: Broaden Slack search in #finance for the other three liability themes so "internal discussions" in the prompt is satisfied beyond StormCloud. Use conversations_search_messages with channel filter for finance and queries such as "Mosaic" or "DOT" or "Heartland" or "PHMSA" or "HM-2026". Capture any incremental status color from Marcus, Alejandro, or Hana that email and QuickBooks alone did not surface.
OE 13: Resolve addressing and channel ids for the required outbound actions. Use contacts_search_contacts for "Marcus Thorne" and confirm marcus.thorne@moveops.com. Use channels_list or workspace docs pattern to confirm finance channel id C005 for hashtag finance posts.
OE 14: Send the reconciliation summary email with send_email from hana.kim@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC only if the prompt or policy requires it, default none unless user later adds). Body must integrate all four workstreams with explicit numbers: Mosaic contingent exposure uses $35,000 net uncovered MoveOps exposure per OE 2b while acknowledging $90,000 CM and $50,000 vendor layer still booked until Rachel signs, DOT accrual $50,000 with June 5, 2026 deadline context from the extension, Heartland hold revised to $3,200 with Marchetti SLA reasoning and brief mention of the three disputed names, include Jake's 60 day Q3 payment term extension, StormCloud $500 with EST versus INV status from QuickBooks. This is a write action verified from the trajectory tool parameters.
OE 15: Post a team update in finance Slack using conversations_add_message with channel_id C005. Payload should summarize at least three of four items (Mosaic, DOT, Heartland, StormCloud) with enough specificity that finance readers see amounts or document ids, matching the reconciliation conclusions from OE 1 through OE 12.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata
